name: Rainier, Frances
description: What memories Oh dear What memories.

name: Noble, Waite
description: Noble Waite is never late To keep a date with June To da da da, to da da da We hope it'll happen soon.

name: Harms, Arthur A.
description: There was a young man named Harms Who had the most wonderful charms A hand made marcel made all the girls yell But they soon learned 'twas all false alarms.

name: Thomas, E. Buckman
motto: "Head awhirl, Who has vamped him? That damn girl."

name: Wall, Alice
description: Alice is like a fair sweet dream, She's just too good to be true. I'll bet she'd nearly cry If a handsome lad said boo.

name: Valentine, Olds
description: Made up of a roly poly build, and a very confident air, She takes as much interest in Manzanita as though she were matron there.

name: Higgins, Virginia C.
description: Virginia Higgins, she's known as Gin. If instead of drinking so much of it She only drank enough of it, She'd be better than she's ever been.

name: Bartlett, Leo
description: Leo Bartlett is grad from here, They say he's a Helluvan Engineer. He's gone to Oakland to win his fame, And also the hand of his fairy dame.

name: Malone, George W.
description: In baseball and football Molly's a bear In boxing too you should see him tear But lately they say in a friendly bout A lightweight named Cupid knocked him out.

name: Organ, Hallie Reno
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Cabinet Secretary (3)
description: Her scholarship is A-1 now; Twasn't so good last year, For how can anyone study hard When Al, sweet Al, is near.

name: Metscher, E. August
description: Besides in his schoolwork Metscher has claim To a great deal of honor In the stud poker game.

name: Sullivan, Louise
motto:
"Oh Boy ain't it grand
To have a guy with a car!
A strong major in Home Ec,
And a line that goes far."

name: Whalman, Harold F.
description: Harold F. Whalman, they call him "doc," He just came back this year; He's as steady as an eight-day clock Except when he's full of—coffee.

name: John, Quigley
description: Have you ever heard of Quigley, The one that's christened John? Without a doubt, he's as gay a scout As ever had a collar on.

name: Wogan, Helen
description: In a day that will be by and by, Of Helen we will say: We knew this famous authoress At U. of N. so gay.

name: Russell, L. Boardman
description: Russell is a mountaineer, He travels round both far and near. He's always rushing, but where he goes We've often wondered, nobody knows.

name: Wooster, Earl
description: Earl Wooster—the Bohemian Duke Could talk an awful lot; His voice was fine and he sure did shine, But what he said did not.

name: Burke, Emily
description: Emily Burke with her vampish eyes, She smiled at me one day; And I've never been the same it seems As before she smiled that way.

name: Chatfield, Charles M.
description: Charlie was a silent lad, Quiet and studious as can be Until he became a senior And fell for women, especially Dorothy.

name: Edward, Benson
description: Has won an ogre's fame With a battle cry of "Tub 'em all" He tries to live up to his name.

name: Hawkins, Lulu
description: Lulu has talent without any doubt, She really is quite hard to beat. To singing and dancing, dramatics and art Add vamping, and it is complete.

name: Carter, William L.
description: Bill is long and lean and tall, In fact he overtops them all. He knows too much about the ed, So we'd better leave the rest unsaid.

name: Gelmstedt, Earl V.
description: Gelmstedt with hair so red, I'll tell you why he's here He wants to be, it seems to me, An Electrical Engineer.

name: Badger, Enola
description: Enola Badger, so I've heard, Fell in love with a gay old rooster; The head is bald of this old bird, So I guess it must be Wooster.

name: Gavin, Yater
description: Gavin is a youngster, Of Agricultural fame; But he has one serious failing, He steps out with a dame.

name: Cory, M. Maurice
clubs: B. S. Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: Quiet and Somber, Demeanor Stern; With the women He has lots to learn.

name: William E. Melarkey
motto: "Will we ever work a problem Like the one we worked in strength That was the night sweet William On the ground you laid your length."

name: Egan, George R.
description: Censored.

name: Douglas, John M.
description: John M. Douglas, the A. T. O. Wonders where the shekels go, That he gathers in from business ads And turns over to the Sagebrush lads.

name: Anne, Underwood
description: When she came to college Demure little Anne Who'd have guessed she'd vamp her A pious young man.

name: Fuss, Helen
description: Auburn tressed and full of pep, Seldom stirs a muss; If into trouble you must get, Why go to Helen Fuss.

name: William, Martin
description: Willie Martin goes a-courting Almost every year. By his female he will hover Like a wooing, cooing lover While she whispers, "Baby dear."

name: Rose, E. Harris
clubs: Editor Y. W. C. A. Edition Sagebrush (3)

name: Codd, Lois
description: Lois Codd it seems quite odd, You don't stay long at college. But then my dear, we sometimes hear, Of other roads to knowledge.

name: Sloan, Leila
description: Leila Sloan traveled alone While here at the U. of N.; She's stepping now, sweet cookie wow! She don't have to be in at ten.

name: Morris, T. Smith
description: M. T. Smith with a Roman nose Is a bear on his number nines. He swings and slides wherever he goes, And for his heart a fair one pines.

name: John R. Bryan
motto: "What can you say when a man's married? He already has trouble enough. We'll say he's a darn good worker and omit the rest of this stuff."
clubs: Transfer from Colorado School of Mines

name: Humphreys, Adelaide
clubs: Vice-President and Secretary D. A. E. (4)
Editor Y. W. C. A. Edition of Sagebrush (4)
description: Full of pep. Always talking, Likes to step.

name: Dunkle, Gladys
description: Gladys Dunkle of all the girls We love her to distraction. We love her eyes, her auburn curls And her winsome, wily action.

name: Smythe, Lois
description: Earnest and persevering, Always doing her share As sweet as they could possibly make her, This describes Lois fair.

name: Dallas, Vera
description: Quiet and unassuming, Yet with lots of charm; Seldom called to use it, Else she'd cause lots of harm.

name: Gottardi, John
description: John should be a ladies man For the ladies all adore him. Instead he goes his own sweet way, He says the ladies bore him.

name: Griffin, Robert B.
description: Las Vegas' pet, Nevada's joy, Be kind to him nature, Keep him always a boy.

name: Brown, Editha
description: The boys all want to know Why Dede learned to wrestle From all appearances she was built In a manly bosom to nestle.

name: Murray, Hazel
description: Hazel is so dignified, For she has taught you see, She knows lots more than most of us, 'Cause she takes chemistry.

name: Frank, Philip R.
description: He's too full of something now; He's as short and plump and full of fun, As a placid Jersey cow.

name: Eldon, Witwer
description: Eldon's an Aggie, Perhaps that's enough; Before long he'll shovel Lots of this stuff.

name: Readle, Wilma
description: Wilma Readle, I love her just a little, She's been a good old pal. And Wilma she loves Benny And that suits me—like hell.

name: Booth, Beulah
motto:
"Just a cozy little home
With only room for two,
A sewing machine and a gas plate
To make Beulah's dreams come true."

name: Walsh, Francis
description: Francis Walsh—you gay old rebel, I'll bet you're as full of Irish blood As I am full of the devil.

name: Frances, Jones Reno
description: Frances may be little, Indeed she is quite small. But when it comes to jazz and pep Frances leads them all.

name: Braun, Thelma
clubs: Junior Y. W. C. A. Cabinet (2)
description: Thelma, Thelma, how I love you Poets sang in days of yore And this little phrase Herb echoes And his echo, we encore.

name: Abad, Leopoldo F.
description: Leo hails from across the sea Where all they wear is part of a tree In the summer when the weather's fine That's O. K. but in autumn time?

name: Leslie, Bruce
description: 'Tis the third year that he's been here, And still he hasn't fallen; But a woman's wiles with sidelong smiles, And I'll bet he won't need calling.

name: Ronnow, Lynne C.
description: He hails from Panaca; Juice is his hobby, Poker his game.

name: Marienne, Elsie
description: Oh hear the bell, we love it well Also the girl who rings it Her praises long would make a song 'Tis Jimmy Shaver sings it.

name: Rainier, Thalia
description: A miss most bewitching. Her name is Rainier. She came from Ohio. We're glad that she's here.

name: Luce, Harvey
description: Harvey Luce, there's nought but goose That rhymes with your last name Alas of you, goose is not true Through brains you won your fame.

name: Perl, A. Decker
description: Decker, Decker, you gay old wrecker Of many a happy home. With hair so blonde and eyes so blue You can't be let alone.

name: Wickland, Vera
description: It seems that Vera grows younger, More baby-like all the while. Her bobbed hair and actions are charming, And who can resist her big smile.

name: Walker, Evelyn
clubs: Chairman Junior Y. W. C. A. Cabinet (2)
description: Brown haired, dark eyed, Petite brunette, Look out Eddie, Mel'll get her yet.

name: William D. Conrad
description: Connie's a queener of note, Every night he steps out With them thin or stout, But to date only one has his goat.

name: Zeni, Anthony
description: Language whiz, debating shark. And we're quite sure of this; If our guess we don't miss, In this world he'll leave his mark.

name: Murray, Louella
description: Louella has taught school it seems, In days not long gone past. The faculty all fondly hope Her dignity will last.

name: Ross, Dorothy
description: Comes from Mills Co-education Gives her thrills.

name: Woodfred, E. Romig
description: Romig, they've named you wrong, And that I ought to know. You are so young and slim and long, It should be Romeo.

name: Frost, Jack
description: John Austin Frost, they call him Jack, He won his fame on the cinder track. Slim and lean like a spring-time fawn, He's a charming lad to look upon.

name: Soren Christenson
description: We've often found Chris feeling blue He wondered if she were true So we gave him a dime, for a merry old time And down to Sparks he went to see Sue.

name: Thompson, Rowene
description: Transfer from U. C. (3). Spit-curl Thompson some call her, But we prefer Rowene, 'Cause that's the name most fitting For our California queen.

name: Tobias, David
description: From New York comes Tobias. If his mines pan out Without any doubt He'll have enough money to buy us.

name: Harris, Gertrude G.
description: Gertrude Harris, fair and tall, Promenades with Hugo; And when we've figured all in all, We wonder why you do so.

name: Beamer, Mary Reno
description: How does your garden grow I'm not worrying now about spade or plow For you see I've got Ernest in tow.

name: John, Wall
description: For the Fallon oil wells Johnny fell, Invested his capital, worked like hell; The oil was missing, the money too, He missed a semester, as I guess you knew.

name: Hugo, M. Quillici
description: He shines in the dark in a lonely park With only Gert by his side, But we'd better hush about this mush, Or you'll hear that we have died.

name: Harker, Ernest
description: One night at the Inn, the Silver Pheasant, A surprise was sprung, it was really pleasant, Mary blushed and Ernest was flushed; We may soon be sending a present.

name: Gignoux, Marienne
description: To rhyme with Gignoux we select the word Sioux. If we write any further we'll get in a stioux.

name: Steinheimer, Ethel
description: We can't razz 'em all, We haven't the stuff, And if we know Ethel— But I guess that's enough.

name: Twaddle, Ralph H.
description: A bachelor he's been for many years, But now by his fall we're moved to tears. He steps around with a sweet young lass, But why does he pick on the Freshman class?

name: Herbert, Shirley
description: Too formal to the opposite sex. Too much regard for form. You'd be in much more favor if you'd thaw and be more warm.

name: Harriman, June
clubs: Treasurer and Secretary of Agriculture Club (2)
description: June is as fair as the month, Her heart is full of bliss, But her aspirations are Noble, And many will vouch for this.

name: Smith, Gladys
description: Gladys has bewitching ways A disposition charming, She's loved by girls, she's loved by men, Her tactics are alarming.

name: Reed, Edward C.
description: Eddie Reed a halfback star, He's a wonder wherever you see him At home, in church or in front of a bar, It'd be an honor to me to be him.

name: Jones, Lester C.
description: Since Lester Jones is far away It's safe enough for us to say That all the girls love Lester Jones And over this they make no bones.

name: Johnson, Homer E.
description: He hits 'em low, and he rode the rods from Kansas. But every time we tell him so, he pops right up and slams us.

name: Brown, Norma
description: It seems I've heard of Norma Brown, I met her at a skid. I didn't think she liked the Irish, But now I know she did.

name: Coffin, Arvella M.
description: Just a little Tri-Delt, Jolly and full of fun; With a complexion fair, and a debonair air That keeps all the men on the run.

name: Byrkit, James W.
description: The kindly gods sent Jimmy, From some place in the east. We hope they let him come again. He's good to say the least.
